demographics.living_situation: 3-day-old baby at home with mother and father; both parents present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He is gagging constantly and seems so unsettled; we don't know how to comfort him.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last few hours our baby keeps gagging and has a lot of spit/secretions in his mouth, and he brings up every feed. He was sent home a few days ago for comfort care and we are frightened and don't know what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: the gagging and heavy secretions came on suddenly about 3 hours ago; the feeding problems started around 12 hours ago
symptom_narrative.duration: constant for the last few hours and not settling
symptom_narrative.character: he keeps gagging and grimacing, there is a lot of spit/saliva pooling in his mouth, and he seems uncomfortable and unsettled
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it is very distressing to watch; he seems really uncomfortable and we feel helpless
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his mouth and throat — lots of secretions; and bringing up feeds
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: frequent gagging episodes, more or less continuous over the last few hours
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: we are not sure; nothing we have tried has settled it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it seems worse when we try to feed him — the milk comes straight back up
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: every attempted feed over the last 12 hours has come straight back up (regurgitation)
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has not been like this before since coming home 3 days ago
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is fully dependent on us for everything; he does not move much on his own and has no settled sleep pattern

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: he was born with a serious problem where the top of his head and part of his brain did not form; it was picked up during the pregnancy on a blood test and scans
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: antenatally (during pregnancy), confirmed at birth 3 days ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: he was discharged home a few days ago for palliative (comfort) care with a community palliative care team

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: he has a gap in the roof of his mouth (cleft palate)

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: comfort medicine drops (morphine) that the team prescribed for him

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: both parents are at home and extremely distressed and exhausted; they are grieving and frightened, and are afraid to give the prescribed morphine drops in case they do something wrong

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: born 3 days ago with a major congenital condition; totally dependent, minimal spontaneous movement, no established sleep-wake cycle (as parents describe)

objective_data_offered.value: frequent gagging and grimacing episodes with saliva/secretions pooling visibly in the mouth
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent-held camera; qualitative observation only, not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: his breathing looks fast and a bit irregular, and he brings his milk straight back up after every feed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent observation; no measured respiratory rate or oxygen level available at home

objective_data_offered.value: he feels a normal temperature when we touch him, and we have not seen any jerking or twitching movements
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: no home thermometer reading given; touch-based impression only

objective_data_offered.value: the top of his head did not form fully and he has a gap in the roof of his mouth, as we were told at birth
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent restating the known antenatal/birth diagnosis, not a new finding
